---
title: "Session timetable"
order: 1
comments: false
---

This is the **nowcasting** course, taught in the first half of the SISMID week.
It is paired with the companion [forecasting & evaluation course](https://nfidd.github.io/sismid-forecasting/).

The schedule follows the standard SISMID day: modules run 09.00–10.30, 11.00–12.30, 13.30–15.00 and 15.30–17.00, with coffee breaks at 10.30 and 15.00 and lunch 12.30–13.30.
Timings below are a proposed plan, to be confirmed.

# Day 1 — Monday 13 July: Epidemiological delays

## Session 0: Introduction and course overview

Monday: 09.00–10.00

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-the-course-and-the-instructors): the course and the instructors
-   [Getting started](../getting-started): getting set up and checking installations work
-   [Introduction to the course](../sessions/introduction-and-course-overview): how the course is taught
-   [Motivating the course](../sessions/slides/from-line-list-to-decisions): from an epidemiological line list to informing decisions in real-time

## Session 1: Delay distributions

Monday: 10.00–10.30 and 11.00–12.30

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-epidemiological-delays): epidemiological delays and how to represent them with probability distributions
-   [Practice](../sessions/delay-distributions): simulate and estimate epidemiological delays
-   Wrap up

*Coffee break 10.30–11.00 · Lunch 12.30–13.30*

## Session 2: Biases in delay distributions

Monday: 13.30–15.00

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-biases-in-epidemiological-delays): biases in delay distributions
-   [Practice](../sessions/biases-in-delay-distributions): interval censoring and right truncation
-   Wrap up

*Coffee break 15.00–15.30*

## Session 3: Using delay distributions to model the data generating process of an epidemic

Monday: 15.30–17.00

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/convolutions): using delay distributions to model the data generating process of an epidemic
-   [Practice](../sessions/using-delay-distributions-to-model-the-data-generating-process-of-an-epidemic): implementing a convolution model and identifying potential problems
-   Wrap up

# Day 2 — Tuesday 14 July: Estimating transmission and nowcasting

Tuesday: 09.00–09.15 — Day 1 review

## Session 4: $R_t$ estimation and the renewal equation

Tuesday: 09.15–10.30

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-reproduction-number): the time-varying reproduction number
-   [Practice](../sessions/R-estimation-and-the-renewal-equation): using the renewal equation to estimate $R_t$
-   Wrap up

*Coffee break 10.30–11.00*

## Session 5: Nowcasting concepts

Tuesday: 11.00–12.30

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-nowcasting): nowcasting as a right-truncation problem
-   [Practice](../sessions/nowcasting): simulating delayed reporting and building simple nowcasting models
-   Wrap up

*Lunch 12.30–13.30*

## Session 6: Nowcasting with an unknown reporting delay

Tuesday: 13.30–15.00

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-joint-estimation-of-nowcasting-and-reporting-delays): joint estimation of delays and nowcasts
-   [Practice](../sessions/joint-nowcasting): the reporting triangle and joint estimation of delays, nowcasts and $R_t$
-   Wrap up

*Coffee break 15.00–15.30*

## Session 7: Jointly fitting multiple data streams

Tuesday: 15.30–17.00

-   [Introduction](../sessions/multiple-data-streams): combining multiple data sources by jointly fitting the renewal model to several parallel streams
-   [Practice](../sessions/multiple-data-streams): fitting cases, deaths and wastewater together, and exploring data conflict
-   Wrap up

# Day 3 — Wednesday 15 July: Bridging to forecasting and real-world methods

Wednesday: 09.00–09.15 — Day 2 review

## Session 8: Combining nowcasting and forecasting

Wednesday: 09.15–10.30

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/forecasting-nowcasting): why combine nowcasting and forecasting?
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecasting-nowcasting): connecting nowcasting and forecasting
-   Wrap up

*Coffee break 10.30–11.00*

## Session 9: Summary and discussion

Wednesday: 11.00–11.30

-   [Summary](../sessions/slides/closing) of the course
-   Final discussion and [further reading](../sessions/end-of-course-summary-and-discussion)

## Session 10: Methods in the real world

Wednesday: 11.30–12.30

-   [Methods in the real world](../sessions/methods-in-the-real-world): presentations and discussion on real-world uses of nowcasts and reproduction number estimates
